topic: We moved into a house that was suspiciously cheap and now I know why
title: We Moved Into a House That Was Suspiciously Cheap and Now I Know Why | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: We bought the house on Aldermere Street for sixty thousand under asking.
scenes.image_prompt: exterior wide shot of a two-story wood frame house on a quiet suburban street at dusk, porch light off, FOR SALE sign still in the front yard with a SOLD sticker across it, neighboring houses dark, slight fog, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The realtor said the previous owners relocated quickly.
scenes.image_prompt: interior wide shot of an empty living room with wood floors and white walls, afternoon light through sheer curtains, a single cardboard box left in the corner, dust on the windowsill, no furniture, vertical 9:16 framing

interior shot of a dining room with a table and four chairs still set as if for a meal, plates and cups left on the table, afternoon light casting long shadows across the surface, no people, dust visible in the light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Three months in, my wife noticed the backyard photographs.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a row of framed photographs hanging on a hallway wall, each showing the same backyard from the same angle in different seasons, the photos slightly crooked, dim hallway lighting, one frame with a cracked glass, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Same yard. Different families. Every decade back to the fifties.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close up of a single framed photograph on a wall showing a family standing in a backyard in front of a wooden fence, the photo aged and yellowed, the people dressed in nineteen sixties clothing, dim interior light, vertical 9:16 framing

close shot of four framed photographs mounted in a row on a hallway wall, each showing different groups of people standing in the same outdoor space, dim light, the expressions in all photos obscured by age and shadow, vertical 9:16 framing

close shot of hands holding a manila folder of photocopied county property records on a kitchen table, overhead lamp casting warm light, papers slightly overlapping, handwritten notes in the margin, dim surrounding room, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Eleven owners in forty years. Each stayed under two.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a typed property transfer ledger with dates and names in two columns, a hand pointing at one entry with a pen, the paper aged and slightly coffee-stained, pool of lamp light on the table, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last winter I found a photograph behind our bedroom mirror.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of the back of a large wall mirror leaning against a wall, a single photograph taped to the backing with yellowed tape, the photo facing outward, dim bedroom light, the mirror frame dusty, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Our backyard. Our car in the driveway. Taken from inside the house. We had owned it for three weeks.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a Polaroid photograph lying on a wood floor, showing a view through a window of a backyard and the rear of a dark sedan in a driveway, the photo slightly overexposed, the window frame visible at the edges, dim light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: We listed it that same month.
scenes.image_prompt: exterior wide shot of the two-story house with a fresh FOR SALE sign in the yard, morning light flat and grey, one upstairs curtain slightly parted, no people visible outside, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: It sold in four days. Sixty thousand under asking.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a FOR SALE sign in a front yard with a SOLD sticker applied diagonally across it, the sticker slightly crooked, grass overgrown at the base of the sign post, overcast morning light, vertical 9:16 framing
